text.title: VUI Privacy Principles and Trust Indicators
text.summary: Five privacy principles for voice products and mandatory trust indicators that give users visible control over voice data collection.

rule.statement: Disclose what voice data is collected, how it is used, and for how long — before the user first speaks, not buried in terms.
rule.rationale: Transparency is both a regulatory requirement (GDPR Art. 13) and a user trust baseline; late disclosure causes opt-out and product abandonment.

rule.statement: Collect only the audio and transcript data necessary for the immediate task; do not retain raw audio for analytics by default.
rule.rationale: Data minimization reduces regulatory exposure and breach impact; users are more willing to use voice features when they know data is not persisted indefinitely.

rule.statement: Encrypt all voice data at rest and in transit; use envelope encryption with per-device keys for recordings.
rule.rationale: Voice recordings are biometric-adjacent data; a breach without encryption exposes users to identification and voice-cloning attacks.

rule.statement: Provide a user-accessible voice history view backed by a separate auditable store; agents must fetch it, never reconstruct or summarize from LLM context.
rule.rationale: If the agent can summarize history from context, it can hallucinate it; a separate store ensures accuracy and supports GDPR/CCPA right-of-access requests.

rule.statement: Implement a hard-delete endpoint for voice history that removes raw audio, transcripts, and derived embeddings; confirm deletion to the user.
rule.rationale: GDPR Art. 17 right to erasure applies to voice data; embeddings derived from voice are biometric data with a separate retention clock.

rule.statement: Show a visual indicator (LED ring or on-screen icon) whenever the microphone is active; provide an audio confirmation when it stops.
rule.rationale: Users cannot see whether a microphone is active; a visual/audio indicator is the primary signal that gives users confidence they are not being recorded covertly.

rule.statement: Make the "stop listening" command work without a wake word and without TTS acknowledgement; provide a silent, immediate kill switch.
rule.rationale: A stop command that requires hearing the wake word first defeats its purpose; users in sensitive situations need an immediate, unconditional off switch.

rule.statement: For banking, health, and personal data operations: require step-up authentication, mask spoken sensitive values, and confirm before executing.
rule.rationale: Voice is inherently ambient; a second channel (push notification or SMS) that must be acknowledged prevents voice-spoofing attacks from completing high-stakes actions.

rule.statement: Treat voice biometric IDs as biometric data under GDPR/CCPA — separate consent, separate retention clock, separate deletion API.
rule.rationale: Biometric data has heightened regulatory protection; co-mingling it with regular user data risks using the wrong retention period and deletion scope.
